page: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes hyper-threading, a 35 MB L3 cache, and support for AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI vector extensions. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 1229 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark evaluations place this server consistently in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yielded 29822.96, while the single-core score reached 1421.79. Memory bandwidth tests showed a read-only small block cached speed of 1663.77 GB/sec, dropping to 107.43 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression represents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% tier at 23.52 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieved an average tier result of 131.5 GB/sec.

With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage, the D32ds_v4 offers a stable resource density for general-purpose enterprise workloads. It is suited for relational databases, static web serving, and moderate software compilation tasks. While it can handle small to medium LLM inference tasks, such as 7B model text generation at 18.25 tokens/sec, it exhibits weak performance on larger 70B models due to the lack of hardware acceleration. The hardware profile represents a standard mid-tier cost-to-performance ratio for non-GPU workloads requiring local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, 1229 GB of local SSD storage, and supports Premium Storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, its x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instructions. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average tier, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 29822.96 and a static web serving throughput of 21.18 GB/sec. However, single-threaded gzip compression is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation, offering a balanced resource profile for workloads that do not require GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, substantial local SSD storage, and mid-tier multi-core compute capabilities.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8272CL processors at 2.6 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory paired with 1229 GB of local SSD storage.
Supports AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets for vector processing.
Delivers average-tier performance for database operations and static web serving.
Exhibits single-threaded compression bottlenecks in gzip benchmarks.
Suited for relational databases, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
